content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Greedy Merchants". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/178/problem/B3
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of items. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the prices of the items.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort the prices in descending order and calculate the maximum possible profit by considering the maximum number of items that can be bought and sold.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) if sorting is done in-place, O(n) otherwise

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
max_profit = 0
for i in range(n - 1):
    if arr[i] < arr[i + 1]:
        max_profit += arr[i + 1] - arr[i]
print(max_profit)
